resources.string: This prototype uses AI to screen banana leaf images for possible nutrient-deficiency symptoms. It is not a replacement for laboratory testing or expert agricultural advice.
Use future screening results as model suggestions with uncertainty, not field decisions.
Prepare a clear banana leaf photo before starting image screening.
Use one banana leaf, good lighting, minimal shadow, and fill the frame with the affected leaf area.
Photo capture will be connected in a later ticket.
Gallery selection will be connected in a later ticket.
This shell does not decode images or run model screening.
This app is being built for classroom and research review of on-device banana leaf nutrient screening.
Model output can be uncertain and should be reviewed with field context.
Visual symptoms can overlap across nutrient stress, lighting, crop age, and image quality.
Confirm with expert inspection or laboratory/soil testing.
Do not use this prototype to choose field treatments or application rates.
Model label order: boron, calcium, healthy, iron, magnesium, manganese, nitrogen, phosphorous, potassium, sulphur, zinc.
Nitrogen and phosphorous are maize-derived supplemental classes and are not banana-validated.
